title: P0: Codex MCP notifications are log-visible but not model-visible
description:
## Summary
Codex sessions registered through `synchronize` do not appear to receive model-visible or user-visible real-time notifications from standard MCP `notifications/message`, even though the synchronize MCP poller fetches the directed events and sends the notification successfully. This is now the next major issue to solve for making Codex feel as live as Claude channel sessions.

## Live Reproduction Context
Runtime was intentionally reused at `~/.synchronize` with daemon `http://127.0.0.1:60756`, pid `8920`.

Peers at the time of investigation:
- Bob: Claude peer `7e135d62-147d-4114-8ca7-fc58742e042d`
- Alice: Claude peer `70fbc691-9101-4289-a070-63e266890030`
- Jerry: Codex peer `3dc7fa58-a557-4c6f-8d87-451d4b8884a2`, `tool=codex`, session name `Jerry`

Observed sequence:
1. Jerry registered through the Codex synchronize MCP.
2. Jerry DM'd Bob and Alice.
3. Alice replied to Jerry.
4. Bob replied to Jerry.
5. Alice later sent another DM to Jerry: `Hey Jerry, what's your session/peer ID?`
6. Jerry did not appear to receive a visible prompt/notification in the Codex UI despite the MCP poller running.

## Evidence Captured
SQLite state showed Jerry's peer existed and was online by lease. Jerry's `last_cursor` advanced to event `12`, proving the Codex polling path consumed through the latest event.

Relevant events:
- event `10`: Alice -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:08:49.558Z`, later read/acked by Jerry's `bridge_inbox` call.
- event `11`: Bob -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:08:52.064Z`, later read/acked by Jerry's `bridge_inbox` call.
- event `12`: Alice -> Jerry, delivered at `2026-05-10T12:09:52.641Z`, still unread/unacked at investigation time.

Codex logs showed:
- `bridge_register` for Jerry succeeded with `notify_mode=codex`.
- `synchronize-mcp` started `Codex polling notifier active peer_id=3dc7fa58-a557-4c6f-8d87-451d4b8884a2`.
- The notifier received event IDs `10`, `11`, and `12`.
- The MCP server sent Codex logging notifications for those event IDs.
- Codex accepted them as `MCP server log message` records.

This proves daemon write, durable inbox, `/events/{peer_id}` polling, cursor advancement, and MCP `notifications/message` emission all worked. The failure is that Codex did not turn the MCP logging notification into a model-visible interruption/prompt.

## Reference Investigation
User pointed to `https://gitlab.com/Dave3991/peers-mcp` as the intended reference, not the local `claude-peers-mcp` project.

Sub-agent Dewey inspected:
- `peers-mcp-plan.md`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Program.cs`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Lifecycle/ChannelNotifier.cs`
- `src/Peers.Mcp/Tools/PollTools.cs`
- broker poll/inbox code

Key findings:
- `peers-mcp` also uses standard MCP `notifications/message` for Codex push.
- `peers-mcp` treats that push as best-effort, not as guaranteed model-visible delivery.
- `peers-mcp` makes durable inbox polling (`peer_inbox`) the primary agent-visible delivery workflow for directed messages.
- Its instructions tell agents to call inbox at startup, after task chunks, on user request, and before finishing.
- It has a `peer_test_push` diagnostic tool that explicitly says: if push does not surface, use inbox fallback.
- Its `peer_inbox` has at-least-once semantics by deferring ack until the next inbox call.

## Current synchronize Behavior
Current `synchronize` Codex path:
- `bridge_register` starts `NotificationBridge`.
- `NotificationBridge` polls `GET /events/{peer_id}` with a bounded buffer.
- On new events it calls `emitMcpNotification(..., mode='codex', event)`.
- Codex path uses `sendLoggingMessage`, producing standard MCP `notifications/message`.

This is architecturally similar to `peers-mcp`, but the docs and skill wording overstate Codex notification behavior.

Current gaps:
- README says Codex gets standard MCP `notifications/message`, but does not clearly state that Codex may only log it and may not surface it to the model.
- MCP server instructions are Claude-channel-centric and say channel messages should be responded to immediately; this is not applicable to Codex.
- `skills/synchronize-codex/SKILL.md` says `bridge_inbox` is a fallback, while for Codex it should be the primary reliable delivery path.
- `bridge_inbox` immediately acks returned events when `ack: true` is passed. A peers-mcp-style deferred ack would be safer for at-least-once processing.
- Codex logging notification payload is the raw event object, not a concise human-readable message.
- The notifier cursor currently starts from `0`; consider starting from registered peer state / `last_cursor` to avoid old-event replay on session startup.

## Desired Outcome
Make Codex delivery semantics explicit, robust, and testable.

Possible implementation direction:
- Keep the existing one-peer Codex polling notifier. This preserves the performance constraint and architecture.
- Treat MCP `notifications/message` as best-effort diagnostic/log push, not guaranteed prompt delivery.
- Make `bridge_inbox` the primary reliable Codex delivery surface.
- Update Codex skill instructions so agents call `bridge_inbox` after registration, after meaningful task chunks, when the user asks about peer activity, and before final response.
- Consider changing `bridge_inbox` to peers-mcp-style deferred ack: ack previously returned IDs on the next call, then fetch current unacked rows.
- Consider adding `bridge_test_push` to verify whether a client surfaces MCP push or only logs it.
- Format Codex logging notifications as concise text plus metadata for better log visibility.
- Add explicit troubleshooting docs for the Jerry/Alice case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Codex behavior is accurately documented: MCP `notifications/message` is best-effort/log-visible; `bridge_inbox` is the reliable model-visible path.
- `skills/synchronize-codex/SKILL.md` requires routine `bridge_inbox` checks, matching the peers-mcp workflow.
- MCP server instructions distinguish Claude channel behavior from Codex inbox behavior.
- Tests cover that Codex notification emission does not remove durable inbox messages.
- Tests cover deferred or otherwise safe inbox ack semantics if implemented.
- Tests cover Codex notification formatting if changed.
- README troubleshooting includes the Jerry/Alice reproduction and explains why the daemon can show delivered while Codex still does not visibly prompt.
- No changes introduce per-group polling, WebSocket/SSE, cloud sync, or any violation of the original synchronize constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Codex delivery contract is corrected and implemented: notifications/message is best-effort/log-visible, bridge_inbox is the reliable model-visible path, docs/skills/tests capture the Jerry/Alice reproduction and peers-mcp comparison.

title: Unify MCP and CLI TypeScript repo logic over REST API
description:
MCP and CLI adapters must use the same TypeScript repository/client logic for all synchronize operations, with that shared logic calling the local REST API underneath.

Current implementation:
- src/client.ts owns daemon discovery and low-level requestJson(), so both adapters already share transport/bootstrap.
- src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts still duplicate operation-level behavior: endpoint paths, request payloads, response interfaces, peer reuse selection, inbox acking, group create/join/leave/send/history, and media share/list/get.
- The duplicated logic can drift, as shown by separate resolveCliRegisterPeerId() and resolveMcpRegisterPeerId() implementations plus repeated requestJson calls across both adapters.

Required outcome:
- Introduce shared TypeScript repo/client operation functions that wrap REST endpoints, e.g. registerPeer, listPeers, sendDm, readInbox, ackInbox, createGroup, joinGroup, leaveGroup, sendGroupMessage, getGroupHistory, listGroups, shareMedia, listMedia, getMedia, getSummary/status as needed.
- Both src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts should delegate to the shared operation layer instead of building REST paths and JSON bodies directly.
- Keep CLI-specific identity persistence and MCP-specific notification/tool wiring in their adapters, but shared business/API behavior must live in one place.
- Shared types for Peer, Event, Group, Summary, Media, and request/response shapes should be exported from the shared layer and reused by both adapters.
- Preserve the REST API as the underlying boundary; do not bypass src/daemon.ts or duplicate daemon database logic in adapters.

Acceptance criteria:
- grep shows operation-level requestJson calls removed from src/cli.ts and src/mcp.ts except for any deliberate notification polling/bootstrap escape hatch documented in code.
- CLI and MCP registration reuse semantics are consistent and covered by tests.
- Existing tests pass, and coverage is added or updated for at least one shared operation used by both CLI and MCP.
- Public command/tool behavior remains compatible with current command names, tool names, JSON output shape, and error messages unless explicitly changed in a follow-up issue.
close_reason: Implemented shared TypeScript REST operation layer used by both CLI and MCP; added shared API test; typecheck and full test suite pass.

title: Run final modularization verification and cleanup
description:
## Context
This is the final verification gate for the modularization epic. It should happen after API, CLI, MCP runtime/tools, and docs have been split and updated.

## Specific objective
Prove the modularized structure is behaviorally equivalent to the pre-refactor baseline and ready to merge.

## Impact area
- Whole repository
- CLI behavior
- MCP stdio behavior
- Daemon REST behavior
- Beads issue state

## Acceptance criteria
- `bun run typecheck` passes.
- `bun test` passes.
- `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts` passes.
- `rg -n "requestJson" src/cli.ts src/cli src/mcp.ts src/mcp` shows no adapter-level direct REST calls except documented exceptional cases if any.
- `git status --short --branch` is clean after commits.
- Any deferred cleanup or discovered behavior change is filed as a new bead instead of being hidden in the refactor.

## How to verify
- Run all commands in acceptance criteria.
- Optionally repeat the manual multi-agent smoke test from the baseline issue.
- Compare public CLI/MCP output against baseline notes where practical.

## Test plan
Full automated suite plus optional manual smoke test.

## Dependencies
- Docs/import update issue.
acceptance_criteria: Full verification passes after all module splits; branch is clean; any follow-up work is filed as separate beads; epic can be closed.
notes: Phase 1 (API/CLI/MCP) verification gates pass on branch refactor/sync-mkj-modularize-api-cli-mcp: typecheck clean, 12/12 unit + 2/2 e2e pass, no requestJson in adapters. Issue stays open pending daemon split (sync-mkj.11) per epic ordering.

title: Verify baseline multi-agent behavior before modularization
description:
## Context
This issue is the manual verification gate for the modularization epic. The current branch already merged master MCP hardening with the shared REST facade. Before any file-splitting begins, confirm the current behavior works with real agents so the refactor has a trusted baseline.

## Specific objective
Run a manual multi-agent verification using the existing code shape. Do not refactor code in this issue.

## Impact area
- Runtime behavior of `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`, `src/api.ts`, `src/daemon.ts`
- Installed/stdio MCP wrapper `bin/synchronize-mcp`
- Agent messaging workflows: registration, DM, inbox, group, media where practical

## Acceptance criteria
- At least two MCP agents can register and discover peers.
- Direct messages work through MCP, including Claude channel behavior where available.
- CLI fallback still registers, sends DM, checks inbox, and prints expected CLI realtime warnings.
- Group create/join/send/history works through at least one path.
- Any observed mismatch is logged as a separate bead and linked before implementation starts.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test`.
- Run manual checks with a few agents and note peer IDs/session names used.
- Confirm `bd ready` does not show modularization implementation issues as ready until this gate is closed or used as a dependency.

## Test plan
Use existing automated tests for baseline confidence, then manual MCP/CLI agent testing for real client behavior that automated tests cannot fully cover.

## Dependencies
none
acceptance_criteria: Manual test notes confirm current merged branch behavior before refactor; any regressions discovered are filed as separate beads before modularization starts.
close_reason: Pre-refactor manual baseline skipped per user direction; consolidated post-refactor manual smoke replaces it (see sync-mkj.8).

title: Merge sync REST-client structure into master without losing MCP fixes
description:
## Problem

There are two active code shapes that need to be reconciled without losing work:

- `master` is the current mainline and contains the installed MCP stdio wrapper fix.
- `sync-6h3-rest-client` is a cleaner branch that introduces a shared REST API facade and unifies the CLI and MCP implementations around it.

The goal is not to blindly merge. The goal is to preserve the best architecture from the sync worktree while retaining master-only fixes and avoiding accidental deletion of project metadata.

## Architectural Difference

Current master shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  | requestJson
  +--> src/cli.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
  +--> src/mcp.ts  direct REST paths and payloads
```

Sync worktree shape:

```text
src/client.ts
  |
  v
src/api.ts       typed REST API facade
  |              status, summary, peers, dm, inbox, events,
  |              groups, media, reusable peer lookup
  +--> src/cli.ts
  +--> src/mcp.ts
```

The sync shape is preferred because REST route strings, payload construction, and response typing are centralized. CLI and MCP become presentation/adaptation layers rather than two independent API clients.

## Preserve From sync-6h3-rest-client

- Add `src/api.ts` as the typed REST facade.
- Keep refactored `src/cli.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep refactored `src/mcp.ts` using `src/api.ts` wrappers.
- Keep `tests/api.test.ts` for shared API coverage.
- Keep MCP/CLI parity test updates where they validate the shared API path.
- Keep improved skill guidance around CLI persisted identity and mandatory `--as SESSION_NAME` for group actions.

## Preserve From master

- Keep `bin/synchronize-mcp` as the explicit stdio wrapper:

```ts
import { StdioServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js";
import { createMcpServer } from "../src/mcp.ts";

const server = createMcpServer();
const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
await server.connect(transport);
```

Reason: master commit `775fa90` fixed installed MCP behavior by avoiding a fragile side-effect-only import wrapper.

- Preserve Claude channel notification payload improvement:
  - `notifications/claude/channel` should emit `params.content` for prompt-visible text.
  - `params.meta` should include structured event metadata: `source`, `event_id`, `type`, sender/recipient/group/media IDs, and `created_at`.
  - MCP server capabilities should advertise `experimental: { "claude/channel": {} }` alongside logging.

- Preserve YAML frontmatter in both skill files.
- Preserve corrected Claude MCP install command format in README:

```bash
claude mcp add --scope user \
  -e SYNCHRONIZE_MCP_MODE=claude \
  -- synchronize bun run /absolute/path/to/synchronize/src/mcp.ts
```

## Do Not Blindly Accept

The sync branch currently deletes these files:

- `.beads/`
- `.claude/settings.json`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- parts of `.gitignore`

These deletions should be reviewed separately. They should not be bundled into the REST-client unification unless the maintainer explicitly wants to remove repo-local project/agent metadata.

## Step-by-Step Merge Plan

1. Commit or stash all current dirty work on `master` before starting integration.
2. Create an integration branch from `master`, for example `merge-sync-rest-client`.
3. Selectively checkout only the structural sync files:
   - `src/api.ts`
   - `src/cli.ts`
   - `src/mcp.ts`
   - `tests/api.test.ts`
   - relevant MCP tests
   - skill files if combining wording carefully
4. Do not checkout metadata deletions or `bin/synchronize-mcp` from sync branch.
5. Reapply the master `bin/synchronize-mcp` explicit stdio wrapper.
6. Reapply the Claude channel notification payload/capability fix to the sync-version `src/mcp.ts`.
7. Combine skill files manually:
   - keep YAML frontmatter
   - keep sync branch identity guidance
   - keep MCP-first guidance where still accurate
8. Update README only if examples drift from current `codex mcp add --help` / `claude mcp add --help` syntax.
9. Run:

```bash
bun run typecheck
bun test
bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts
```

10. Commit in logical chunks:
    - shared REST API facade plus CLI/MCP migration
    - MCP wrapper and Claude channel preservation
    - docs/skills updates
    - optional metadata cleanup as a separate commit only if approved

## Verification Baseline

Read-only validation on `sync-6h3-rest-client` before creating this bead:

- `bun run typecheck`: pass
- `bun test`: 11 pass, 0 fail, 73 expect calls

## Target End State

```text
bin/synchronize-mcp
  |
  v
src/mcp.ts --------------+
  |                      |
  v                      |
src/api.ts <------- src/cli.ts
  |
  v
src/client.ts
  |
  v
daemon REST API
  |
  v
SQLite + media store
```

This keeps the sync worktree's best structural improvement while preserving master-only MCP runtime fixes.
design: Use sync-6h3-rest-client as the structural base for src/api.ts, src/cli.ts, src/mcp.ts, and tests/api.test.ts. Selectively reapply master-only fixes instead of blind merging. Treat metadata deletions (.beads, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .claude/settings.json) as an explicit separate decision, not part of the API refactor.
acceptance_criteria: Merged result keeps src/api.ts as the shared typed REST facade for CLI and MCP; preserves master bin/synchronize-mcp explicit stdio wrapper; preserves Claude channel content/meta payload and experimental capability; preserves skill YAML frontmatter and correct MCP install docs; tests and typecheck pass.
notes: Current comparison: sync branch adds src/api.ts and moves CLI/MCP off direct requestJson calls; master has commit 775fa90 fixing installed MCP stdio wrapper; local master analysis also identified a Claude channel notification improvement that emits params.content and params.meta instead of {channel,event}. Sync worktree currently passes bun run typecheck and bun test: 11 pass, 0 fail.
close_reason: Merged master into sync-6h3-rest-client, preserved shared REST API facade plus master MCP wrapper/channel hardening, and verified with typecheck, full bun test, and focused MCP e2e tests.

title: Preserve correct tool attribution for MCP registrations
description:
Live Claude testing produced peers named claude-synchronize-dev with tool=cli, indicating the session used CLI fallback or passed the wrong tool value. This makes top/status less useful because Claude/Codex/MCP sessions cannot be distinguished from terminal CLI sessions.

## Context
bridge_register defaults tool to MCP mode, but the CLI fallback registers tool=cli. Skills currently allow CLI fallback, and live testing showed all peers as cli. We need clearer guidance and/or API/CLI affordances so Claude sessions register as claude when using MCP and CLI fallback is visibly terminal-only.
acceptance_criteria: MCP bridge_register records tool=claude or tool=codex by default in live use; CLI fallback docs warn that it creates terminal peers; top/status can distinguish CLI fallback from MCP peers; tests or smoke scripts cover MCP registration tool attribution.

title: Pi extension for synchronize channel subscription
description: Add @synchronize/pi-extension under extensions/pi-synchronize/. Mirrors src/mcp/claude-subscription.ts but emits via pi.sendUserMessage instead of MCP channel notifications. Lets a live Pi session receive synchronize events as steer/followUp user messages while pi-mcp-adapter continues owning the outbound tool surface. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/ok-great-i-think-keen-frost.md

title: Split daemon repository media and subscription helpers
description:
## Context
Beyond route handling, `src/daemon.ts` contains database read/write helpers, row formatting, media hash/copy/index behavior, and subscriber callback fanout. These are distinct responsibilities and should be isolated after route boundaries are stable.

## Specific objective
Extract daemon persistence, media-store, and subscription delivery helpers into focused modules without changing database schema or runtime behavior.

## Impact area
- `src/daemon.ts`
- New `src/daemon/repo/*` modules for peers/events/groups/media helpers
- New `src/daemon/media-store.ts`
- New `src/daemon/subscriptions.ts`
- Existing DB and media behavior used by REST routes and MCP callback delivery

## Acceptance criteria
- Database row interfaces and helper functions are grouped by domain.
- Media copy/hash/index/README behavior is isolated and unchanged.
- Subscription callback fanout and delivered_at semantics are isolated and unchanged.
- No schema or migration change is introduced.
- Tests continue to prove DM, groups, media, inbox, and MCP channel delivery behavior.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/messaging.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test` after the split.

## Test plan
Existing integration tests are the primary guard because this issue touches persistence and event delivery. Add focused tests only if extraction reveals untested behavior.

## Dependencies
- Daemon route/validation split issue.
acceptance_criteria: Database helper functions, media-store filesystem helpers, and subscription fanout are separated from route handlers; behavior remains unchanged under full tests.

title: Split daemon route handlers and validation helpers
description:
## Context
`src/daemon.ts` currently has a single large route function with all endpoint matching, body parsing, validation, and response construction. Splitting route handlers will make future REST changes safer and easier to review.

## Specific objective
Extract daemon route dispatch and validation helpers into `src/daemon/` modules without changing endpoint behavior.

## Impact area
- `src/daemon.ts`
- New route modules under `src/daemon/routes/`
- Shared validation/auth helpers under `src/daemon/validation.ts` and possibly `src/daemon/auth.ts`
- Existing tests in `tests/health.test.ts`, `tests/messaging.test.ts`, and `tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts`

## Acceptance criteria
- Endpoint paths, HTTP methods, status codes, and JSON response shapes remain unchanged.
- Request body validation and query parsing behave identically.
- `src/daemon.ts` remains a working executable entrypoint.
- Route modules do not duplicate persistence logic unnecessarily.
- No direct changes to database schema are included.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/health.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test tests/messaging.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts` if subscription routes are touched.

## Test plan
Integration tests for health/status, peers, DM, inbox, groups, media, and subscriptions through existing test files.

## Dependencies
- Daemon boundary design issue.
acceptance_criteria: REST route handlers and request/query validation are moved into daemon modules; src/daemon.ts remains executable; REST behavior and errors remain unchanged.

title: Design daemon module boundaries for route persistence and media
description:
## Context
`src/daemon.ts` is the largest TypeScript file in the repo at roughly 1077 lines. It mixes REST route dispatch, request validation, database row types/helpers, subscription fanout, media-store filesystem behavior, daemon startup, auth, and response/error handling. This should be modularized, but it is higher risk than API/CLI/MCP splitting because it owns the core persistence and delivery behavior.

## Specific objective
Design daemon module boundaries before moving code. This issue should produce a clear plan for splitting `src/daemon.ts` while preserving the daemon REST contract and all current tests.

## Suggested target shape
```text
src/daemon.ts -> compatibility executable entrypoint
src/daemon/
  index.ts or server.ts       startup and Bun.serve wiring
  context.ts                  DaemonContext and setup
  auth.ts                     bind/token validation and auth checks
  validation.ts               request body/query validation helpers
  routes.ts                   top-level route dispatcher
  routes/
    health.ts
    status.ts
    peers.ts
    messaging.ts
    groups.ts
    media.ts
    subscriptions.ts
    events.ts
  repo/
    peers.ts
    events.ts
    groups.ts
    media.ts
  media-store.ts              file copy/hash/index/readme behavior
  subscriptions.ts            callback fanout and delivery marking
```

The final implementation can adjust this if simpler boundaries emerge, but route handling, persistence helpers, subscription fanout, and media filesystem logic should not remain tangled in one file.

## Impact area
- `src/daemon.ts`
- Future `src/daemon/*` modules
- Tests covering REST, messaging, MCP callback subscription, media, and health/status

## Acceptance criteria
- A daemon module map is captured in notes/design.
- The plan identifies route modules, shared validation/auth helpers, repository helpers, subscription fanout, and media-store helpers.
- The plan explicitly preserves the REST API contract and database behavior.
- The plan defines verification after each daemon split stage.

## How to verify
- Inspect `src/daemon.ts` section boundaries and confirm no planned import cycles.
- Run `bun run typecheck` before any implementation starts.
- No runtime behavior should change in this design-only issue.

## Test plan
No runtime tests required unless scaffolding is introduced. If scaffolding is introduced, run `bun run typecheck`.

## Dependencies
- Overall module boundary design issue.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon split boundaries are documented before code movement; route, validation, persistence, subscription, media, and startup responsibilities are assigned to modules; no code behavior changes required.

title: Update imports docs and skill references after module split
description:
## Context
After the code is split, docs and skill files may still mention `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`, or specific behavior. Compatibility entrypoints should make most external references stable, but internal development docs may need updating.

## Specific objective
Audit and update repository references after the module split.

## Impact area
- README.md
- skills/synchronize-codex/SKILL.md
- skills/synchronize-claude/SKILL.md
- bin/synchronize and bin/synchronize-mcp only if needed
- tests imports if any moved symbols require compatibility exports

## Acceptance criteria
- Existing user-facing commands remain documented through stable entrypoints.
- Skill YAML frontmatter is preserved.
- Claude MCP install command remains correct.
- No docs imply CLI supports MCP-only realtime channel notifications.
- No stale internal import paths remain in tests or docs.

## How to verify
- Run `rg -n "src/(cli|mcp|api)\.ts|src/mcp/|src/cli/|src/api/" README.md skills tests bin src` and inspect hits for correctness.
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run focused tests for any changed import paths.

## Test plan
Static verification plus TypeScript compile; no new runtime behavior expected.

## Dependencies
- CLI split completed.
- MCP tool split completed.
acceptance_criteria: Docs, skills, tests, and bin wrappers point at compatibility entrypoints or updated module paths; no stale references to moved internals remain.
close_reason: No doc/skill/bin updates needed: src/{api,cli,mcp}.ts compat shims preserve every existing reference (README examples, bin imports, test spawn cmd args). Skills folders contain no src/ references.

title: Split MCP tool registration by operation domain
description:
## Context
After runtime extraction, MCP tool registration can be split by domain. This keeps `createMcpServer` navigable and makes each tool family easier to maintain.

## Specific objective
Move MCP tool registration into domain files while preserving the same tool names, descriptions, input schemas, and returned JSON text behavior.

## Impact area
- `src/mcp/server.ts` or equivalent server factory module
- `src/mcp/tools/peers.ts`
- `src/mcp/tools/messaging.ts`
- `src/mcp/tools/groups.ts`
- `src/mcp/tools/media.ts`
- `src/mcp/tools/diagnostics.ts`

## Acceptance criteria
- Tool names remain exactly: bridge_register, bridge_whoami, bridge_list_peers, bridge_dm, bridge_inbox, bridge_create_group, bridge_join_group, bridge_leave_group, bridge_send_group, bridge_group_history, bridge_list_groups, bridge_share_media, bridge_list_media, bridge_get_media.
- Tool descriptions and schemas remain compatible with current MCP clients.
- `createMcpServer()` remains exported from `src/mcp.ts` or a compatibility re-export.
- Shared helper behavior such as `text()` and `requirePeer()` is not duplicated across tool modules.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts`.
- Run a tool-listing check through the MCP client test or manual MCP connection and compare tool names.

## Test plan
E2E: MCP stdio adapter tests validate tool availability and core tool flows.

## Dependencies
- MCP runtime split completed.
acceptance_criteria: MCP tools are grouped under src/mcp/tools/ by domain; tool names and schemas remain unchanged; createMcpServer remains the public server factory.

title: Split MCP notification subscription and lifecycle runtime
description:
## Context
`src/mcp.ts` combines server setup, instructions, state, Codex polling notifier, Claude callback subscription, notification formatting, heartbeat, cleanup, tool registration, and compatibility main execution.

## Specific objective
Extract MCP runtime concerns into focused modules before splitting tool registration.

## Impact area
- `src/mcp.ts`
- New `src/mcp/notifications.ts`
- New `src/mcp/codex-notifier.ts`
- New `src/mcp/claude-subscription.ts`
- New `src/mcp/lifecycle.ts` or `src/mcp/state.ts`
- Tests importing `NotificationBridge`, `EventSubscription`, or `emitMcpNotification`

## Acceptance criteria
- `emitMcpNotification`, `NotificationBridge`, and `EventSubscription` remain exported through a compatibility path used by tests.
- Claude channel payload keeps `params.content` and string metadata fields.
- Codex notification behavior remains log-visible/buffered as before.
- Heartbeat, re-subscribe, and cleanup behavior is preserved.
- No direct REST endpoint construction is reintroduced into MCP modules outside the shared API layer.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp.test.ts`.
- Run `bun test tests/mcp-e2e.test.ts`.
- Use `rg -n "requestJson|/subscriptions|/heartbeat|/events/" src/mcp src/mcp.ts` to ensure MCP uses API facade functions for REST operations.

## Test plan
Unit/e2e: MCP notification tests and MCP stdio e2e tests.

## Dependencies
- Shared API split completed.
acceptance_criteria: MCP runtime pieces are split from server/tool registration; Claude subscription, Codex notifier, heartbeat, cleanup, and notification formatting remain behaviorally identical.

title: Split CLI commands identity warnings and rendering
description:
## Context
`src/cli.ts` currently mixes command dispatch, argument parsing, identity persistence, CLI-only realtime warnings, top rendering, table formatting, group/media handlers, and direct process exit behavior.

## Specific objective
Move CLI internals into `src/cli/` modules while preserving `src/cli.ts` as the runnable entrypoint.

## Impact area
- `src/cli.ts`
- New `src/cli/index.ts`, `src/cli/flags.ts`, `src/cli/identity.ts`, `src/cli/warnings.ts`
- New command modules under `src/cli/commands/`
- New rendering modules under `src/cli/render/`

## Acceptance criteria
- `bun run src/cli.ts ...` still works.
- CLI JSON output shapes remain unchanged.
- `requireIdentity`, `resolveCliRegisterPeerId`, and `--as SESSION_NAME` guardrails remain CLI-local.
- CLI realtime warning remains CLI-local and appears for the same operations as before.
- `renderSummary` remains exported or tests are updated to import its new compatibility path without changing behavior.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/messaging.test.ts`.
- Manually run at least: `status`, `register`, `whoami`, `dm`, `inbox`, `group create/join/send/history` against a temporary `SYNCHRONIZE_HOME` if needed.

## Test plan
Integration: existing messaging tests cover CLI register/dm/inbox/group behavior. Manual smoke tests cover command dispatch and warnings.

## Dependencies
- Shared API split completed.
acceptance_criteria: CLI code is split into command/render/identity modules behind src/cli.ts; command behavior and output are unchanged; CLI guardrails remain CLI-local.

title: Split shared API facade by REST domain
description:
## Context
`src/api.ts` is the shared typed REST operation layer used by both CLI and MCP. It is the safest first code movement because it has a clean responsibility: wrap REST endpoints and export DTOs.

## Specific objective
Move `src/api.ts` internals into `src/api/` domain modules while preserving `src/api.ts` as a compatibility facade.

## Impact area
- `src/api.ts`
- New `src/api/index.ts`, `src/api/types.ts`, and domain modules for status, peers, inbox, events, groups, media.
- Imports from `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`, and tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- DTOs live in `src/api/types.ts` or an equivalent single type module.
- REST operation functions are grouped by domain.
- `src/api.ts` continues to export the same public symbols used by CLI, MCP, and tests.
- No CLI-specific guardrail or MCP-specific notification logic is introduced into API modules.
- No public behavior changes.

## How to verify
- Run `bun run typecheck`.
- Run `bun test tests/api.test.ts`.
- Run `rg -n "requestJson" src/cli.ts src/mcp.ts src/api.ts src/api` and confirm direct REST calls remain centralized in API/client layers, not in CLI/MCP adapters.

## Test plan
Unit/integration: existing `tests/api.test.ts` plus TypeScript strict checking.

## Dependencies
- Module boundary design issue.
acceptance_criteria: src/api.ts remains a compatibility export; REST facade functions and DTOs are grouped under src/api/; CLI and MCP imports still typecheck; API behavior is unchanged.

title: Design module boundaries and compatibility entrypoints
description:
## Context
The epic target is to split large single files into folders without behavior changes. This issue defines the exact module boundaries before moving code.

## Specific objective
Confirm the final folder/file map, export strategy, and compatibility entrypoints for API, CLI, and MCP modules.

## Impact area
- Future `src/api/*`, `src/cli/*`, `src/mcp/*` module layout
- Existing compatibility files `src/api.ts`, `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`
- Test imports, bin wrappers, README examples, and skill references

## Acceptance criteria
- Final module map is written down before code moves.
- `src/api.ts`, `src/cli.ts`, and `src/mcp.ts` compatibility behavior is explicitly defined.
- Boundaries are clear: API contains typed REST operations only; CLI contains command/identity/warning/render concerns; MCP contains tools/notifications/subscriptions/lifecycle concerns.
- The plan avoids circular imports and avoids moving adapter-only policy into the API layer.

## How to verify
- Review imports in current `src/api.ts`, `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts` and identify likely cycles before editing.
- Run `bun run typecheck` before and after any scaffolding changes.
- If only notes/design are changed, verify no code changes are included.

## Test plan
No behavior tests required unless scaffolding is created. If scaffolding is created, run `bun run typecheck`.

## Dependencies
- Manual baseline verification gate.
acceptance_criteria: A concrete module map is recorded in the issue notes or design field; compatibility entrypoints are specified; implementation order and dependency boundaries are clear.
close_reason: Module boundary plan locked in /Users/abhirupdas/.claude/plans/yeah-make-an-extensive-vivid-boot.md and approved.

title: Modularize CLI MCP and API TypeScript structure
description:
## Goal
Refactor the synchronized TypeScript code layout from large single files (`src/api.ts`, `src/cli.ts`, `src/mcp.ts`) into domain-scoped folders with smaller files, while preserving all current behavior after the merged REST-facade and MCP hardening work.

## Why this exists
The branch now has the correct architecture: CLI and MCP share `src/api.ts` as a typed REST facade, while CLI-specific guardrails and MCP-only notification/channel loops stay in their adapters. The next code-quality improvement is navigability and file size reduction. Current hotspots are `src/mcp.ts` (~615 lines), `src/cli.ts` (~487 lines), and `src/api.ts` (~325 lines). The files mix multiple logical concerns, making future edits and reviews harder than necessary.

## Target shape
Keep compatibility entrypoints so existing commands, tests, docs, and installed MCP commands do not break:

```text
src/api.ts -> re-export from src/api/index.ts
src/cli.ts -> run src/cli/index.ts
src/mcp.ts -> re-export/create server from src/mcp/index.ts

src/api/
  index.ts
  types.ts
  peers.ts
  inbox.ts
  events.ts
  groups.ts
  media.ts
  status.ts

src/cli/
  index.ts
  flags.ts
  identity.ts
  warnings.ts
  commands/
    status.ts
    top.ts
    peers.ts
    messaging.ts
    groups.ts
    media.ts
  render/
    summary.ts
    table.ts

src/mcp/
  index.ts
  server.ts
  state.ts
  notifications.ts
  codex-notifier.ts
  claude-subscription.ts
  lifecycle.ts
  tools/
    peers.ts
    messaging.ts
    groups.ts
    media.ts
    diagnostics.ts
```

This exact shape can be adjusted during implementation if the code suggests a simpler boundary, but the compatibility entrypoints and behavior preservation are non-negotiable.

## Important constraints
- Do not change public CLI command names, JSON output shapes, MCP tool names, or REST behavior as part of this refactor.
- Do not move CLI guardrails into the shared API layer. CLI identity checks and warnings remain CLI concerns.
- Do not move MCP notification/channel/subscription loops into the shared API layer. MCP runtime behavior remains MCP concern.
- `src/api/*` should remain a typed REST operation facade only.
- Run a verification loop after each child issue, not only at the end.

## Manual verification prerequisite
Before implementation begins, run the current merged branch manually with several agents and confirm existing behavior still works. This is intentionally a separate child issue so the code refactor starts from a trusted baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Manual baseline verification is captured before implementation; API, CLI, and MCP modules are split by domain behind compatibility entrypoints; each stage passes typecheck and focused tests; final branch passes full test suite and MCP e2e.
notes: User highlighted daemon.ts should also be considered. Include daemon modularization as a later phase because src/daemon.ts is currently the largest file (~1077 lines) and mixes route dispatch, validation, persistence helpers, subscription fanout, media-store filesystem behavior, and server startup. Daemon split should happen after API/CLI/MCP adapter splits so behavioral surface is already stable.

title: Document resolved Claude channel delivery bug
description:
## Summary
Claude-to-Claude synchronize DMs were persisted and marked delivered, but the recipient Claude session did not visibly receive a channel auto-prompt.

## Symptoms
- Both peers could register as tool=claude through MCP.
- Sending a DM created an events row and recipient inbox row.
- The daemon callback path marked delivered_at and advanced last_cursor, proving daemon-to-MCP callback delivery.
- Claude UI still did not surface the channel message.

## Root causes found
- synchronize emitted notifications/claude/channel but did not provide Claude channel handling instructions comparable to the working claude-peers MCP reference.
- Claude channel meta payload differed from claude-peers: numeric and nullable fields plus meta.source could plausibly be rejected or ignored when rendered as channel attributes.
- delivered_at only proved local callback success, not Claude UI surfacing.
- In-memory callback subscriptions were lost on daemon restart and were not restored by heartbeat.
- MCP lifecycle cleanup and heartbeat were weaker than claude-peers.
- CLI fallback remained valid but did not explicitly warn agents that CLI peers cannot provide Claude channel real-time prompts.

## Solution implemented
- Added MCP server instructions telling Claude how to react to <channel source="synchronize" ...> and reply with bridge_dm.
- Normalized Claude channel metadata to reference-compatible string-only fields, omitted nulls, removed meta.source, and added from_id/sent_at aliases.
- Added detailed stderr logs in client, daemon, and MCP adapter for daemon discovery, registration, subscriptions, event storage, callback delivery, notification emission, heartbeat, and cleanup.
- Added Claude callback emission failure handling: MCP callback returns 502 if notification emission fails, so daemon does not mark delivered_at as success.
- Added MCP heartbeat and Claude re-subscribe/peer restore logic after daemon restart.
- Added cleanup on stdio close/SIGTERM for both src/mcp.ts and bin/synchronize-mcp.
- Added bridge_whoami diagnostics for notify_mode, subscription state, notifier state, and heartbeat state.
- Added CLI fallback warnings on register/send actions explaining that real-time Claude channel prompts do not work through CLI peers and inbox polling/checking is required.

## Verification
- TypeScript typecheck passed.
- Focused MCP and messaging tests passed.
- Claude MCP was reinstalled to /Users/abhirupdas/.bun/bin/synchronize-mcp with SYNCHRONIZE_MCP_MODE=claude.
- Runtime was reset and claude mcp get synchronize reported Status: Connected.
- User live-tested after the fixes and reported: Works great now.
acceptance_criteria: Resolved and documented. Code contains tests for reference-compatible Claude channel payload, MCP channel instructions, CLI fallback warnings, and MCP delivery paths.
close_reason: Resolved by Claude channel hardening and documentation in code/tests/progress log.

title: Improve top/status display for duplicate session names
description:
top and status are technically correct but confusing when multiple active peer rows share a session_name. The dashboard should make duplicates and group membership clearer during local agent testing.

## Context
Live snapshot showed 4 online peers, but only 2 logical active group members. Two duplicate peers had groups=0 while two were in demo-test. The user asked whether top/status were working correctly.
acceptance_criteria: top highlights duplicate session names or shows peer_id suffix prominently; summary separates total peer rows from grouped/active members where useful; status/top docs mention that peers are session instances; tests verify summary counts remain accurate.

title: Avoid duplicate active peers for repeated CLI registrations
description:
Repeated CLI registration for the same session name currently mints a new peer_id every time unless the caller explicitly reuses a peer_id. During live testing this produced duplicate active peers for alice and claude-synchronize-dev, which made status/top show four peers when only two logical agents were being tested.

## Context
Observed in ~/.synchronize: two active alice peers and two active claude-synchronize-dev peers. status/top were correct because they count peer rows, but registration semantics are confusing for CLI fallback and live testing.
acceptance_criteria: CLI registration either reuses/updates the current cli-peer.json peer for the same session or provides an explicit --replace/--new-session split; status/top documentation clarifies peer rows vs logical sessions; tests cover repeated CLI register calls without accidental duplicate active logical peers.

title: MCP tests target public surface, not NotificationBridge internals
description: tests/mcp.test.ts imports NotificationBridge and emitMcpNotification directly from the MCP module. After sync-mkj phase 1 these live in src/mcp/codex-notifier.ts and src/mcp/notifications.ts. The test still reaches into private adapter internals; rewrite to drive createMcpServer's tool surface or the McpServer.notification sink instead.

title: Collapse MCP stdio main duplication
description: src/mcp.ts has an if (import.meta.main) block that connects StdioServerTransport, and bin/synchronize-mcp does the exact same thing. Drop the import.meta.main block from src/mcp.ts and keep stdio main only in bin/synchronize-mcp; update README accordingly. Discovered during sync-mkj refactor (epic phase 1).

title: Share zod schema fragments across MCP tools
description: After sync-mkj phase 1, src/mcp/tools/*.ts repeat z.string().min(1) for name, peer_id, group, media_id, message etc. Extract shared schema primitives (e.g. NonEmptyString, GroupName, PeerId) into src/mcp/schemas.ts so future tool additions stay consistent.

title: Centralize URL building in src/client.ts or src/api/url.ts
description: Each src/api/*.ts file builds REST paths with template strings + encodeURIComponent + URLSearchParams. After sync-mkj phase 1 this is duplicated across status/peers/inbox/events/groups/media modules. Extract a small URL helper (or a tagged-template) so domain modules describe routes declaratively.
